title: Claude Code to Codex MCP config migration checklist

remote_invariant.notes: Use this artifact as a public checklist only. Do not paste MCP configs, generated memories, API keys, GitHub handles, local paths, or file contents into the hosted Bring Your AI endpoint.

checks.question: Did every MCP server get classified by transport, command or URL, environment references, wrapper dependencies, and target criticality?
Did credential material stay behind environment, Keychain, 1Password, or vault references instead of becoming literal config values?
Were source-machine absolute paths rewritten to target-valid wrappers or recorded as validation notes?
Did each critical MCP server pass tools/list or an equivalent lowest-risk discovery call after migration?
Were Claude Code hooks, launcher policy, and per-agent restrictions that Codex cannot enforce recorded as target-side validation notes?
